title: Jahmai Jones hits memorable first home run for Red Sox
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Jahmai Jones hit a two-run home run in his second appearance for the Red Sox at Fenway Park.
Jones' home run tied the game 2-2 against the Tampa Bay Rays on July 18, 2026.
Previously struggling with the Tigers, Jones provides depth for Boston and excels against left-handed pitching.

title: Wilyer Abreu joins elite Red Sox company following his back-to-back multi-HR games
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Wilyer Abreu is the first Red Sox player with back-to-back multi-home run games since Mookie Betts in 2016.
Abreu's consecutive multi-HR games happened at Fenway Park, joining George Scott who achieved it in 1971.
Abreu's performance helped Boston move over .500 in their quest for a 13th consecutive win.

title: Red Sox extend season-altering win streak to 12 games in epic comeback fashion
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Red Sox extended their win streak to 12 games with a comeback victory over the Tampa Bay Rays.
Wilyer Abreu hit two home runs, including a decisive one to overcome a 6-3 deficit in the seventh inning.
Closer Aroldis Chapman secured the win despite a shaky ninth inning, maintaining Boston's hold on a wild-card spot.

title: Report: LeBron James ‘hates’ Celtics, would rank them last on his list of teams to join
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: LeBron James reportedly ranks the Boston Celtics last on his list of potential free agency destinations.
James has historically clashed with the Celtics, facing them frequently as a rival during his NBA career.
James is considering returning to Miami or Cleveland, or potentially joining the Golden State Warriors.

title: Tom Brady busy at Fanatics Fest, from a face slap to showing off his arm
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Tom Brady slapped Logan Paul during a Fanatics Fest panel in NYC, creating a viral moment.
Brady and Paul have a playful ongoing rivalry stemming from a March flag football encounter.
Aside from the slap, Brady showcased his throwing skills and participated in various activities at the event.

title: 4 charged in connection with alleged kidnapping, beating near Nantucket park
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Four men from Nantucket face charges of kidnapping and assault following an attack on a 28-year-old man.
Suspects allegedly tied the victim with an extension cord on Essex Road after an attack captured on video.
Suspects have been released on bail and are scheduled for arraignment; the victim was treated for non-life-threatening injuries.

title: 2 arrested at Logan Airport as ICE activity ramps up
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Maria Rosales and David Ardila were arrested at Logan Airport during ICE operations while attempting to board flights.
Rosales, under DACA protection, faces a 2017 removal order. Ardila's visa issue stems from delayed asylum application processing.
Their attorney reports about 10 recent immigration-related arrests at Logan, emphasizing ongoing legal challenges for their release.

title: 5 movies to watch after ‘The Odyssey’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey' inspires a curated list of five similar films to stream next.
Recommended films include 'Interstellar,' 'Lawrence of Arabia,' and 'O Brother, Where Art Thou?'.
'The SpongeBob SquarePants Movie' is noted for its thematic parallels to Homer's epic tale.

title: Formidable Red Sox extend win streak to 11 games with doubleheader sweep of Rays
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Red Sox extended their win streak to 11 games, sweeping a doubleheader against the Tampa Bay Rays.
In Game 1, Boston achieved a 10-0 victory with standout performances by Jake Bennett and Carlos Narvaez.
Game 2 saw a 5-3 win as Wilyer Abreu hit two home runs, solidifying Boston's climb to a .500 record.

title: Chad Tracy offers updates on several injured Red Sox pitchers
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Pitchers Ranger Suarez and Connelly Early may soon return to the Red Sox after recent injury progress.
Kutter Crawford, Tanner Houck, and Johan Oviedo show positive recovery steps, possibly joining the team this season.
Roman Anthony, Garrett Crochet, Isaiah Kiner-Falefa, and Marcelo Mayer remain sidelined with no recovery updates.
